verdicts.note: The record claims exists='yes' with url='https://cake-com.github.io/pumble-node-sdk/manifest.html'. However, this URL points to Pumble's own app manifest documentation for building Pumble integrations (their SDK). It is NOT a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for Pumble. The evidence shows no dedicated MCP server exists for this app.
The provided URL is Pumble's own SDK documentation page about app manifests, not an MCP server repository or implementation. This should be removed.
Evidence confirms free tier exists but full API/integration access requires Pro or Business paid plans. The 'paid-plan' classification is accurate.

Evidence from pumble.com/learn/pumble/free-plan-explained/ confirms 'Free plan...includes...Plaky and Clockify integrations' (limited native integrations) and pumble.com/learn/pumble/business-plan-explained/ confirms unlimited integrations require paid plans.

Both OAuth2 (authorization.html) and API key methods (api-keys-integration/) are documented in evidence. OAuth2 uses consent screen + token exchange; API keys generated via Pumble API addon within workspace.
Evidence shows REST API endpoints (api-client.html, manifest.html) with methods for messages, channels, files, etc.
Evidence shows REST API covering messages, channels, files, reactions, scheduled messages, user management, search, shortcuts, slash commands, and event subscriptions. 'medium' breadth is reasonable.

Evidence supports 'buildable-with-caveats': API exists and is accessible, but full integration access requires paid plans (not instant free self-serve), and OAuth2 app creation requires manifest/registration process.

Evidence confirms integration access gated by pricing tier (free plan limited to native integrations like Clockify/Plaky; full access requires Pro/Business) and OAuth2 apps require manifest registration process.
Evidence strongly supports all major claims about API, auth methods, and pricing restrictions. Confidence of 0.6 appears conservative; should be higher (0.85+) given clear documentation.

All three flags are supported by evidence: integration access restricted by pricing tier (confirmed), OAuth2 requires app registration via manifest (confirmed), and no official MCP server exists (confirmed - the cited URL was SDK docs, not MCP).

revised.name: Pumble
revised.one_liner: Pumble is a team messaging platform offering a REST API (with Node SDK) supporting OAuth2 app tokens or workspace-generated API keys for bots/messages/channels, with integration access gated by paid plans.

auth_methods.notes: OAuth2 flow via consent screen and POST https://api-ga.pumble.com/oauth2/access to exchange code for access token, used for full apps.
Pumble API addon allows generating API keys/tokens directly within a workspace (x-app-token + token headers) for simpler direct requests.

revised.self_serve_notes: Free tier exists but third-party app/integration access is restricted to a limited set of native integrations (e.g., Clockify, Plaky); full API/app integration access requires upgrading to Pro or Business plan.

api_surface.notes: REST API with SDK (pumble-node-sdk) covering messages, channels, files, reactions, scheduled messages, shortcuts, slash commands, event subscriptions, etc. Also has an app manifest/trigger framework for building integrations.

revised.main_blocker: Full API/integration access is gated behind paid Pro/Business plans, and OAuth2 app creation requires going through Pumble's app manifest/marketplace process rather than instant self-serve key generation.

evidence.supports: Describes x-app-token and token headers required for every API request, and lists API methods/scopes.
Details OAuth2 authorization flow for obtaining user/bot access tokens.
Describes the Pumble API addon allowing users to generate API keys directly within a workspace.
Confirms free plan limits integrations to specific native apps like Clockify and Plaky.
Confirms unlimited/full integration access requires paid Business or Pro plan.
Describes the app manifest and SDK framework used to build Pumble apps/integrations.

flags: Integration/API access restricted by pricing tier
OAuth2 app creation process not fully self-serve instant (requires app registration via manifest)
No official MCP server exists; previously cited URL was Pumble's own SDK documentation
